response.justification: Bogdan has a strong technical foundation with 20+ years of engineering experience and is currently working on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP at re:cinq, which touches some required technologies. However, the resume lacks concrete evidence of the deep AI/ML expertise this role demands: no mention of Python (a required language), vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM optimization, or explicit multi-agent framework experience. The AI work appears recent and vaguely described, and only spans 6 years rather than the required 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions. An interview could clarify depth in these areas, but the gaps suggest he may be better suited for platform or tooling roles rather than a Staff-level GenAI delivery position.
strengths: Extensive TypeScript and full-stack engineering across 20+ years, with proven cloud/DevOps expertise (AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines)
Currently contributing to AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflows at re:cinq, showing recent hands-on exposure to key technologies
Demonstrated ability to build and maintain systems at scale (millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby) and mentor engineers through workshops and open-source leadership
concerns: No Python in skills list despite being a required primary language, and no evidence of AI/ML-specific programming beyond the current role
Resume lacks any mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM performance metrics, or optimization techniques—all explicitly required for this position

AI experience limited to 6 years at re:cinq with vague descriptions of 'AI-agent infrastructure'; does not clearly demonstrate the required 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions or leading technical discovery sessions

key_factors.factor: Insufficient AI/ML expertise depth and duration
Missing Python and vector database/RAG knowledge
Current MCP and agentic workflow experience
